name: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY)
description: Free short-term skill training and certification for youth to improve employability, including recognition of prior learning.
eligibility: Indian youth, typically 15-45 years, who are unemployed or school/college dropouts and want industry-relevant skill training. No fee for the candidate.
benefits: Free training, a government-recognised certificate, and placement assistance.

name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry & Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Financial assistance to early-stage startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market entry.
eligibility: DPIIT-recognised startups incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application, with a business idea using technology, not having received more than Rs 10 lakh of other government funding.
benefits: Grants up to Rs 20 lakh for validation/prototype and up to Rs 50 lakh for market entry via convertible debentures/debt.

name: National Social Assistance Programme (Old Age Pension - IGNOAPS)
description: Monthly pension to elderly persons from below-poverty-line households under the Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme.
eligibility: Persons aged 60 years or above belonging to a below-poverty-line (BPL) household. Pension amount increases after age 80.
benefits: Monthly old-age pension credited via DBT (amount varies by age and state top-up).

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: A low-premium life insurance scheme offering life cover for one year, renewable annually, linked to a bank account.
eligibility: Individuals aged 18 to 50 years holding a savings bank account who give consent to auto-debit the premium.
benefits: Life cover of Rs 2 lakh for a premium of about Rs 436 per year.

name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
description: A very low-premium accidental death and disability insurance scheme linked to a bank account.
eligibility: Individuals aged 18 to 70 years holding a savings bank account who give consent to auto-debit the premium.
benefits: Accidental death/full disability cover of Rs 2 lakh for a premium of about Rs 20 per year.

name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana (Rooftop Solar)
description: Subsidy for installing rooftop solar panels on homes to reduce electricity bills and promote clean energy.
eligibility: Indian households that own a house with a suitable rooftop and a valid electricity connection, and have not already availed a rooftop solar subsidy.
benefits: Central financial assistance (subsidy) covering a substantial part of the rooftop solar system cost, plus free electricity up to 300 units/month.

name: Credit Guarantee Fund for Micro & Small Enterprises (CGTMSE)
description: Provides a credit guarantee to banks so that micro and small enterprises can get collateral-free loans.
eligibility: New and existing micro and small enterprises (manufacturing or service) seeking working capital or term loans, sanctioned without third-party collateral or guarantee.
benefits: Guarantee cover on collateral-free credit up to Rs 5 crore, making bank loans easier to obtain for small businesses.

name: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY)
description: Skill training and assured placement for rural poor youth to enable wage employment.
eligibility: Rural youth aged 15 to 35 years from poor families (with relaxations up to 45 for women and other special groups).
benefits: Free residential/non-residential skill training with certification and guaranteed placement support.
